title: 在密歇根州为智能时代构建基础设施
author: OpenAI
content:
Today, alongside Governor Gretchen Whitmer, local community and labor leaders, and our partners at Oracle, Related Digital, and Walbridge, we broke ground on The Barn⁠(opens in a new window), a 1GW data center campus in Saline, Michigan.

This project is moving forward because we have worked to build it in partnership with the community and because Governor Whitmer has helped create the conditions for Michigan to lead on projects like this. From the beginning, our goal has been to build infrastructure in a way that does right by the people and communities helping make it possible.

That means making a clear set of commitments.

First, local residents will not bear the cost of the infrastructure required to support this project. The infrastructure and energy needed for The Barn will be paid for by the project, and costs will not be passed on to local ratepayers. To be clear, consumers will be protected from paying more in electricity bills because of this project.

Second, we have to protect local water resources. The project is designed to use a closed-loop cooling system that uses about as much water as a typical office building.

Third, this project should create real work and real opportunity for Michigan workers. In Michigan, that means union construction jobs, skilled trades, and a project being built by the kinds of people and companies that know how to do this work well. The Barn is expected to create more than 2,500 union construction jobs in addition to 450 permanent onsite jobs, 1,500 county-wide jobs and an additional 1,000 indirect jobs.

Fourth, projects like this should invest in the places that host them. That is why OpenAI is teaming up with Related Digital, Oracle, Walbridge, and Blackstone to contribute $10 million dollars toward improvements to the Saline Recreation Center, alongside contributions from Oracle and Related. The City of Saline identified the rec center as a community priority, and this investment supports a project shaped by the community itself. The project also is projected to generate $1 billion in tax revenue over the lease term, including support for local, county, and state schools and services in the local community. 

We believe access to AI tools and training should be part of the opportunity created by this investment. That’s why, as part of our broader commitment to Michigan, OpenAI will make up to $45 million in Codex credits available to more than 400,000 eligible Michigan college students, community college students, and trade school students aged 18 and older during the 2026–2027 academic year. Michigan students can access the program at https://chatgpt.com/features/codex-for-michigan-college-students/⁠(opens in a new window). 

We are also partnering with Michigan’s Department of Labor and Economic Opportunity, along with participating community colleges and other state and local partners, on AI literacy and workforce training opportunities focused on practical, workforce-relevant skills.

Together, these efforts are meant to support Governor Whitmer’s broader strategy of helping Michigan students get the education, training, and tools they need for good jobs after graduation.

We know that access to AI tools is a top concern among parents and young people. The concern is driven by the recognition that to be best positioned when it comes to the jobs and economy students will be participating in, they will need AI skills. This program is designed to help prepare these students to be equipped to participate in the AI economy.

These commitments reflect our principle that the communities helping build the infrastructure for advanced AI should share in the opportunities it creates.

That includes labor. Earlier this year, OpenAI announced⁠(opens in a new window) a broader partnership with North America’s Building Trades Unions (NABTU) as part of our infrastructure buildout to help ensure that the construction of AI-related infrastructure supports union careers, strengthens union-registered apprenticeship programs, and creates economic opportunity in the communities where this infrastructure is built.  

Projects like this require thousands of skilled tradespeople and the full strength of the construction workforce needed to build at scale. In a state that helped drive American industrialization in the 20th century, that matters. This kind of work can help catalyze a new era of reindustrialization in Michigan, rooted in skilled labor, serious building capacity, and long-term economic strength.

We also recognize that Michigan has a special place in the American story. This is a state that helped build the industrial heart of the country. The next great era of technology is going to be built in America, and Michigan should help build it.

This project is also part of something bigger. At OpenAI, we’re entering a new phase of our work. We started with research. Then we turned that research into products people use every day. Now we’re helping build the infrastructure that will make advanced AI more useful, more reliable, and more available over time. This project is part of our Stargate program, OpenAI’s long-term effort to build the infrastructure needed to make intelligence more accessible, useful, and reliable for people and businesses around the world.

Compute is becoming one of the real strategic advantages in AI because it helps make intelligence better, cheaper, and more reliable over time. More compute supports better models, and better infrastructure helps bring down the cost of delivering advanced AI to more people and businesses. That is why we moved early and why we are investing across the full stack, from systems and networking to the partnerships and physical infrastructure needed to bring capacity online at scale.

That is also why Michigan matters so much to this work. AI depends on more than software. It depends on energy, construction, supply chains, advanced systems, and the skilled people who know how to build and operate them. Michigan has deep engineering talent, a strong building culture, and a long history of making complicated things real. It should play a major role in building what comes next.

When OpenAI launched Stargate in January 2025, we said this work should support American reindustrialization, create jobs, and generate broad economic benefit. Michigan is showing how that can be done.
aiSummary: OpenAI 宣布在密歇根州萨林市破土动工一个 1GW 的数据中心园区，作为其 Stargate 计划的一部分，并承诺进行本地社区投资、创造工会就业机会以及推动 AI 教育。
oneSentenceSummary: OpenAI 宣布在密歇根州萨林市破土动工一个 1GW 的数据中心园区，作为其 Stargate 计划的一部分，并承诺进行本地社区投资、创造工会就业机会以及推动 AI 教育。
summary: 这篇来自 OpenAI 新闻的文章详细介绍了位于密歇根州萨林市的“The Barn”1GW 数据中心园区的破土动工，这是该公司 Stargate 基础设施计划的关键部分。该项目是与 Oracle、Related Digital 和 Walbridge 合作，并得到了州长 Gretchen Whitmer 的支持。OpenAI 概述了四项核心承诺：保护本地纳税人免受基础设施成本影响、使用闭环冷却系统节约用水、创造超过 2，500 个工会建筑岗位和 450 个永久现场职位，以及向当地社区投资 1，000 万美元，包括萨林娱乐中心。此外，OpenAI 还向超过 40 万名密歇根州学生提供高达 4，500 万美元的 Codex 积分，并与州机构合作开展 AI 素养和劳动力培训。文章将该项目视为美国再工业化的催化剂，利用密歇根州的工业历史和熟练劳动力来构建智能时代的物理基础设施。

mainPoints.point: OpenAI 已在密歇根州萨林市破土动工一个 1GW 的数据中心园区，作为其 Stargate 基础设施计划的一部分。
mainPoints.explanation: 该项目名为“The Barn”，代表了对物理计算基础设施的重大投资，旨在使先进的 AI 更易获取且更可靠。

mainPoints.point: 该项目包括保护本地纳税人、节约用水以及创造数千个工会就业机会的承诺。
mainPoints.explanation: OpenAI 承诺基础设施成本不会转嫁给居民，该设施将使用闭环冷却系统，并将创造超过 2，500 个建筑岗位和 450 个永久职位。

mainPoints.point: OpenAI 正在投资当地社区和 AI 教育，包括为娱乐中心提供 1，000 万美元，以及为学生提供 4，500 万美元的 Codex 积分。
mainPoints.explanation: 该公司正在为社区确定的优先事项做出贡献，并为超过 40 万名密歇根州学生提供 AI 工具和培训，以帮助他们为 AI 经济做好准备。

mainPoints.point: 该项目被定位为美国再工业化的催化剂，利用密歇根州的工业历史和熟练劳动力。
mainPoints.explanation: OpenAI 将该数据中心定位为在美国构建下一代技术的更广泛努力的一部分，密歇根州因其工程人才和建设文化而发挥关键作用。

keyQuotes: 从一开始，我们的目标就是以对人民和帮助实现这一目标的社区负责的方式建设基础设施。
计算正在成为 AI 领域真正的战略优势之一，因为它有助于随着时间的推移让智能变得更好、更便宜、更可靠。
这类工作有助于催化密歇根州新一轮的再工业化，其基础是熟练劳动力、强大的建设能力和长期的经济实力。
AI 不仅仅依赖于软件。它还依赖于能源、建筑、供应链、先进系统以及知道如何构建和运营它们的熟练人员。
